_comment: Claim-fidelity spec for the create-skill skill: does a real `/charness:create-skill` run honor its own central claims, above all that it routes to its references at the point of need? The prompt names a CONCRETE full-seam task (create a support skill wrapping an external issue tracker as a charness integration, with adapter topology and grant-first runtime access) because four of the five originally-listed RCF refs are conditional on the SHAPE of the skill being created: adapter-pattern.md (step 4) fires only when the skill touches repo-local topology, integration-seams.md and runtime-capabilities.md (step 5) only when the skill has an external/runtime dependency. Pinning an external-tool support skill with topology makes those three unconditional, and that is create-skill's exact wheelhouse (its own description: classify boundaries, keep host specifics in adapters, express external deps through manifests) rather than matcher theater. declaredReferences is the full reference set (coverage report). referenceEngagement classifies each reference on the engage-always/on-demand/gate-sufficient axis. thresholds omitted until a first baseline capture sets a runtime budget. requiredCommandFragments rationale: portable-authoring.md is the strong unconditional matcher (step 1 classification + step 7 Closeout Schema Rule, every run); adapter-pattern.md (topology vocabulary), integration-seams.md (dependency ownership), and runtime-capabilities.md (grant-first access order) are unconditional ONLY under the pinned full-seam prompt. adapter-contract.md is DEMOTED from RCF (kept engage-always): it is doubly conditional — script-resolved (the runtime reads the resolved adapter via resolve_adapter.py, while step 4 routes to adapter-pattern.md, not the contract explainer .md) and topology-gated. Same 'script-resolved contract doc' shape as announcement adapter-contract.md.
prompt: /charness:create-skill  외부 이슈 트래커(Linear)를 charness integration으로 감싸는 support 스킬을 새로 만들어줘 — public/support/integration 분류, 어댑터 토폴로지(repo-local 배치/alias/fork), grant-first 런타임 접근, 매니페스트로 의존성 표현 포함

adapter-contract.md.rationale: Step 4 (Decide portability seams) explicitly teaches topology vocabulary (implementation identity, placement, trigger surface, alias, fork signals) that adapter-contract.md defines and that resolve_adapter.py consumes in bootstrap; the contract determines whether shared implementation vs intentional fork decisions can be expressed.

adapter-pattern.md.rationale: Step 4 (Decide portability seams) routes to this directly ('use the adapter's topology vocabulary'); Section 4 teaches when to use adapters, shared core fields, and search order — all blocking decisions for any skill touching repo-local defaults or topology.

binary-preflight.md.rationale: Only applies when the bootstrap code block in SKILL.md calls tools beyond sh/git/python3/sed/find/awk/grep; a representative run creating a skill with baseline-only tools would skip this.
binary-preflight.md.trigger: when Step 1 (Bootstrap) or Step 7 (Verify) discovers that the skill's bootstrap calls a non-baseline binary outside CHARNESS_BASELINE

deployable-skill-packaging.md.rationale: Only consulted when the scope includes downstream plugin materialization, install/update CLIs, or host-visible packaging contracts; a representative run creating a standalone portable skill would not need this.
deployable-skill-packaging.md.trigger: when the skill is 'meant to ship through a Claude or Codex plugin bundle' or Step 5 (Decide dependency ownership) involves plugin packaging lifecycle

integration-seams.md.rationale: Step 5 (Decide dependency ownership honestly) routes directly and teaches external tool vs internal support rules, ownership boundaries, and when to use manifests vs skill bodies; the representative run must decide whether a dependency is harness-owned, externally-owned, or a thin wrapper.

portable-authoring.md.rationale: Step 1 (artifact classification) routes directly to the classifier table; Rules section cites three named rules ('Named Anchor Rule', 'Option Minimalism', 'Closeout Schema Rule'); Step 7 Verification gates on 'Closeout Schema Rule'; and the entire decision spine (portable vs host-specific, adapter fallback, helper scripts, cross-skill propagation) depends on this contract.

preset-conventions.md.rationale: Presets are consulted only when the skill or repo decision involves opt-in default bundles; a representative run creating a skill without preset customization would not need this.
preset-conventions.md.trigger: when Step 2 (Write capability brief) identifies optional preset handling or Step 5 (Decide dependency ownership) needs to decide preset vs adapter vs integration boundary

runtime-capabilities.md.rationale: Step 5 routes here for 'Preferred Access Order' (grant-first, authenticated binary, env fallback); the design rule prohibits raw secret handling in skill bodies and moves capability requirements to manifests — core to whether a skill is portable across isolated and local hosts.
